key_insight: Cayley graphs encode finite-group structure strongly enough that both handcrafted graph statistics and graph neural networks can predict algebraic properties with meaningful accuracy.
engineering_relevance: The paper provides a large, labeled benchmark of 131,406 Cayley graphs that can be used to test graph-based learning methods for symmetry-aware scientific computing and algebraic pattern discovery.
method: Built a dataset covering all groups of order at most 767 except order 512, with exact algebraic labels.
Computed broad graph, cycle, distance, and spectral statistics for each Cayley graph.
Compared classical models, an MLP, and GNNs (notably GIN and some GCN settings) for property prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a census of 131,406 Cayley graphs and evaluation showing that engineered statistics are highly informative, while GIN and some fixed-order GCN models recover substantial structural signal.
watch_item: Performance may depend on graph order and feature engineering, so conclusions about generalization beyond the enumerated group families should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Learning the Graphical Nature of Symmetries
